data.title: Boeing's loss narrows as revenue tops estimates
data.description:
Boeing (BA) rose 3.5% in premarket trading Wednesday after reporting quarterly results that topped Wall Street expectations on revenue and adjusted earnings, as investors weighed improving aircraft deliveries against ongoing geopolitical and travel-market risks.

The planemaker reported first-quarter revenue of $22.22 billion, up 14% from a year earlier and slightly above the Wall Street consensus estimate of $21.91 billion. Adjusted loss was $0.20 a share, better than analyst expectations for a loss of $0.67 a share.

Net loss fell to $7 million, or $0.11 a share, from $31 million, or $0.16 a share, a year earlier.

Before Wednesday, Boeing (BA) shares had climbed 38% over the prior 12 months as investors grew more optimistic that the aerospace giant was regaining operational footing.

The company has worked to overcome safety issues, production scrutiny and supply-chain constraints while airlines worldwide race to secure new aircraft amid strong travel demand. Markets are also looking for management commentary on whether the war in Iran could disrupt deliveries, supply routes or airline spending and whether rising jet fuel prices might pressure carriers and future travel demand.

Cash burn improves

Boeing (BA) reported operating cash flow of negative $179 million, improved from negative $1.62 billion a year earlier. Free cash flow was negative $1.45 billion, also better than the prior-year period.

CEO highlights safety, quality, backlog

“We're building on our momentum with a strong start to the year and growing record-breaking backlog across our business,” Chief Executive Kelly Ortberg said in the earnings release.

He added that Boeing (BA) remains focused on safety and quality while increasing production to meet customer commitments.

Commercial deliveries rise

Boeing (BA) said commercial airplane deliveries increased to 143 in the quarter from 130 a year earlier, helping lift revenue in its largest business segment.

Commercial airplanes revenue rose 13% to $9.2 billion, while the unit’s operating loss widened slightly to $563 million. Boeing (BA) said the 737 program continues producing at a rate of 42 aircraft a month.

The company said it still expects certification of the 737-7 and 737-10 variants in 2026, with first deliveries anticipated in 2027. The 777X program also continued certification progress, with first delivery expected in 2027.

Record backlog supports outlook

Total company backlog rose to a record $695 billion, including more than 6,100 commercial airplanes. Commercial aircraft backlog alone stood at $576 billion.

That order book gives Boeing (BA) long-term production visibility, though investors remain focused on whether the company can steadily raise output and convert demand into cash flow.

data.title: BHP posts lower quarterly production but raises full-year copper guidance
data.description:
BHP (BHP) issued its latest quarterly operational review Wednesday, reporting a 3% Q/Q drop and 7% Y/Y decline in copper production for FQ3, as well as a 10% Q/Q drop but a 2% Y/Y increase in iron ore output.

But shares are indicated up 2.6% pre-market after confirming a long-awaited supply deal with China for its iron ore division.

BHP's (BHP) Q3 copper output of 476.8K tons was slightly below market expectations due to weak performance at the ​Escondida and Pampa Norte operations ⁠in Chile, but WAIO iron ore production on a 100% basis of 69.8M tons came in slightly above estimates.

The miner raised full-year guidance for the copper business, saying it now expects copper production in the year through June in the upper half of its guidance range of 1.9M-2M tons, three months after revising its outlook from an earlier range of 1.8M-2M tons.

It also reduced unit cost guidance at Escondida to $1.00-$1.20/lb from $1.20-$1.50/lb previously, reflecting the increased contribution of by-product credits and strong operational performance.

Full-year guidance for WAIO iron ore production remained unchanged at 284M-296M tons.

BHP (BHP) said earlier this year that copper would account for the largest share of earnings for the first time, overtaking iron ore, which has long been the company's profit engine.

Yet iron ore "did the heavy lifting" in BHP's (BHP) FQ3, and the overall result is "better than it looks, but not clean," RBC Capital analyst Kaan Peker said in a note, highlighting better-than-expected realized prices across commodities, which helped mask a mixed operational outcome.

data.title: Boeing in Charts: Commercial Airplanes revenue up 13% Y/Y in Q1 as deliveries climbed 10%
Alibaba, Tencent may invest in DeepSeek at $20B valuation: report
data.description:
• Chinese tech giants Alibaba (BABA) and Tencent (TCEHY) are in discussions to invest in DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK) at a $20B valuation, The Information reported.
  • Earlier this month, it was reported that DeepSeek had initiated its first fundraising campaign in hopes of raising $300M for a valuation of more than $10B. The additional funds could help DeepSeek hire more AI experts and build more capable models.
  • DeepSeek first came to prominence in January 2024 when it released an AI model that it claimed was created with a fraction of the computing costs of larger, more well-established ones from OpenAI (OPENAI) and Anthropic (ANTHRO). Doubts about the truthfulness of the claim have since been cast later by a significant portion of the tech community.
  • DeepSeek's market share has rapidly declined over the past 12 months. In April 2025, DeepSeek held 10% of the daily average user market share among chatbots, according to a tracker by Apptopia. However, that had declined to 3.3% by March 2026.
  • Alibaba, Tencent, and DeepSeek did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Civista Bancshares GAAP EPS of $0.72
data.description:
• Civista Bancshares press release (CIVB): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.72.
  • Net interest income of $37.8 million, up $5.1 million or 15.4% compared to the first quarter of 2025, and up $1.4 million or 3.8% compared to the fourth quarter of 2025.
  • Net interest margin (tax‑equivalent) expanded to 3.85% during the first quarter of 2026, increasing 34 basis points year‑over‑year and 16 basis points sequentially, reflecting lower funding costs and disciplined balance‑sheet management.
  • Net income, for the first-quarter of 2026 of  $15.0 million, a $4.8 million or 47% increase compared to $10.2 million for the first-quarter 2025, and a $2.7 million or 22% increase compared to $12.3 million for the fourth-quarter 2025.

data.title: Alfa Laval AB reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Alfa Laval AB (publ) press release (ALFVY): Q1 Earnings per share of SEK 4.59 (4.82).
  • Net sales decreased by -3 percent to SEK 15.9 (16.5) billion, with an organic increase of 2 percent.
  • Order intake was SEK 17.6 (17.8) billion, a decline of -1 percent. The organic increase was 6 percent.
  • Adjusted EBITA decreased by -1 percent to SEK 2.9 (2.9) billion, corresponding to a margin of 18.1 (17.7) percent.
  • Cash flow from operating activities amounted to SEK 1.2 (1.4) billion.

data.title: Hamilton Lane launches new interval fund, converts existing fund into interval fund
data.description:
Hamilton Lane (HLNE) launched the new Hamilton Lane Credit Income Fund and converted the Hamilton Lane Private Infrastructure Fund into an interval fund structure.

Hamilton Lane Credit Income Fund was declared effective last month, and has reached more than $350M in commitments.

Hamilton Lane Private Infrastructure Fund was initially launched in October 2024, and is set to continue its existing infrastructure strategy focused on co‑investments and secondaries.

The two interval funds are designed to offer institutional and private wealth investors access to private market strategies.

The features include 1099 tax reporting, quarterly limited liquidity, daily NAV pricing, and an investment minimum of $2,500 in certain share classes.

data.title: Tenax Therapeutics appoints Thomas Staab as chief financial officer
data.description:
• Tenax Therapeutics (TENX) has appointed Thomas Staab as chief financial officer, effective May 11, 2026, replacing Thomas McGauley.
  • Staab brings over 25 years of leadership experience across management and corporate finance roles in the healthcare industry, the company said. He most recently served as CFO and Secretary of LENSAR until May 2026 and Senior Vice President, CFO, and Treasurer at BioCryst Pharmaceuticals from July 2011 to February 2020.
  • TENX +0.88% premarket to $13.8.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: P.F. Chang's eyes going public again in about two years
data.description:
P.F. Chang's appears to be more serious about planning for an IPO in the near future. CEO Jim Mazany said at the Restaurant Leadership Conference that the company is planning to go public in about two years, describing the move more as an internal goal rather than a set plan. He tied the public ambition to the chain's broader turnaround effort, saying the brand is trying to rebuild average unit volumes, lift dining-room sales, and expand both domestically and internationally before filing paperwork with the SEC for an IPO.

Notably, Mazany said he thinks the chain could triple its international store count to 300, according to the Restaurant Business website.

Looking back, P.F. Chang's was founded in 1993 in Scottsdale, Arizona, by Paul Fleming and Philip Chiang, combining Fleming’s restaurant experience with Chiang's family recipes and modern Chinese-American dishes. The chain grew quickly as it became known for signature items like lettuce wraps and wok-cooked dishes before going public in 1998 to set the stage for international expansion. In the 2010s, P.F. Chang’s changed hands through private-equity ownership, spun off its Pei Wei concept, and continued expanding abroad, including openings in China, Mexico City, and Kuwait City.

Other restaurant companies that analysts have circled as IPO candidates include Jersey Mike's, Panera Brands (PANERA), Fogo de Chão, Inspire Brands, 7 Brew, and Café Rio.

data.title: M/I Homes GAAP EPS of $2.55 beats by $0.04, revenue of $920.71M misses by $0.99M
data.description:
• M/I Homes press release (MHO): Q1 GAAP EPS of $2.55 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $920.71M (-5.7% Y/Y) misses by $0.99M.
